diagrams.title: Magnetic Disk Structure
diagrams.description: Cross-sectional view showing platters, spindle, actuator arm, and read/write heads
diagrams.purpose: Understanding physical structure of hard disk drives and how data is organized

diagrams.title: Disk Scheduling Comparison
diagrams.description: Visual comparison showing head movement patterns for FCFS, SSTF, SCAN, and C-SCAN algorithms
elements: Path of head movement for each algorithm
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing how different scheduling algorithms process disk requests and their movement patterns

diagrams.title: SSD Internal Architecture
diagrams.description: Hierarchical structure of NAND flash memory in SSDs
diagrams.purpose: Understanding SSD internal organization and why overwrites are not possible without erase

diagrams.title: RAID Levels Comparison
diagrams.description: Visual representation of data distribution across RAID 0, 1, 5, and 6
elements: RAID 1: Data mirrored on two disks
RAID 5: Data striped with distributed parity
RAID 6: Data striped with dual parity
Parity blocks shown in each configuration
diagrams.purpose: Comparing how different RAID levels store data and provide redundancy
